body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

(Re-evaluation of the 2026-05-18 `partial/partial`, confidence 0.55 assessment.)

## What changed since 2026-05-18

The prior verdict was `agent_supports: partial`, resting on the premise that Claude Code's Agent/Task tool is foreground-by-default and blocks the parent's turn until the child returns — "there is no fire-and-forget send-message-to-a-still-running-background-subagent primitive, so the scenario's defining shape cannot be produced from the CLI." That premise is now FALSE for shipped Claude Code.

The installed binary is `2.1.150`. A `strings $(which claude)` scan surfaces the exact primitive the spec's **Primitive exercised** field names (`subagents` + background dispatch via SendMessage):

- A real `SendMessageTool` with `to:` addressing (`Use SendMessage with to: '<id>'`), plus in-process backends (`InProcessBackend.sendMessage()`, `PaneBackendExecutor`, `[SendMessageTool] In-process teammate ...`).
- An execution-mode taxonomy: `**Execution**: Direct (default), Task agent (straightforward subagents), Teammate (agent with true parallelism and inter-agent communication)`.
- Explicit background dispatch for agents: `run_in_background: true runs the agent asynchronously; you'll be notified when it completes.` and `You can optionally run agents in the background using the run_in_background parameter. When an agent runs in the background, you will be automatically notified...`.
- Teammate liveness semantics that match the scenario: `Idle teammates can receive messages. Sending a message to an idle teammate wakes them up`; idle notifications fire `whenever a teammate's turn ends`.

This is precisely "parent spawns a child that runs without holding the parent open, and the parent dispatches further work via SendMessage." The agent half of the verdict moves to `yes`.

irrlicht's own adapter codebase corroborates the upstream change: `surviveTurnDone` (core/pkg/tailer/tailer_config.go) added `SendMessage` in commit `f15ed63` (2026-04-26) with the comment "Claude Code 2.1.77 replaced Agent({resume}) with SendMessage({to}) for resumption. Same rationale as Agent — the sub-agent runs in the background." So the SendMessage primitive predates even the prior assessment's date; the 2026-05-18 verdict simply under-credited it.

## irrlicht observability (the half that holds at `partial`)

The transport is sound for discovery and linkage: the claudecode adapter is `FilesUnderRoot{transcriptsDir()}` (agent.go), so the fswatcher discovers a background child's `<parent>/subagents/agent-*.jsonl` transcript independently of the parent's turn arc, links it via `ParentSessionID`, and `surviveTurnDone("SendMessage")` keeps the dispatch tool tracked across `turn_done`. The detector has dedicated background-subagent handling (session_detector_subagent.go) with a `SubagentQuietWindow` (30s) guard that explicitly avoids fast-forwarding a child whose transcript is still being written.

But two of the spec's three user-observable signals are NOT met as the daemon ships today:

1. **Parent-not-held is inverted.** The spec's signal #1 is "parent transitions working → ready at turn end; child state is unchanged by parent's transition" — the parent goes `ready` WHILE the background child keeps working. The daemon does the opposite: `reevaluateParent` + `hasActiveChildren` (session_detector_subagent.go) deliberately HOLD the parent in `working` until every linked child finishes. `TestSessionDetector_BackgroundSubagentsNotFastForwarded` codifies this: "parent must be held in working because the child is still active." That is the correct behavior for a foreground subagent, but it is the inverse of the background-subagent signal, so the scenario's distinguishing transition is not observable as authored.

2. **SubagentCount does not exclude the background child.** The spec's signal #3 is `SubagentCount(parent)` excludes the background child. `ComputeSubagentSummary` (core/domain/session/grouped.go) sets `Total = inProcess + len(fileChildren)` and bumps `Working`/`Waiting`/`Ready` for every `ParentSessionID`-linked child — there is NO background-vs-foreground flag on `SessionState` to subtract. A grep of core/ for any background-child distinction in the count comes up empty. So a running background child is counted, not excluded.

Signal #2 (the child's transcript disappears within `readyTTL` after it completes) is observable — orphaned-child promotion + child cleanup handle it.

Net: discovery, linkage, and lifecycle of the child are observed; the two signals that *distinguish* background from foreground (parent goes ready early; count excludes the child) are not produced as-shipped. That is an honest `partial`, not a `yes` — the gap is concrete and in two named code sites, not a defensive downgrade.

## Capability mapping

**Primitive exercised** = `subagents` + background dispatch. The matching key in capabilities.json is `features.subagents: true` (the file uses a flat `features` map, not the per-scenario shape in the SKILL example). That key is already `true` and correct; there is no separate `background_dispatch` feature key in the file, and adding one is a maintainer call, not part of this assessment.

## Matrix drift

The committed coverage matrix (agent-scenarios-coverage.json) already reads `claudecode: yes / partial` with the note "SendMessage continuation; parent-not-held semantics rely on surviveTurnDone list; per-flow validation incomplete." This re-assessment RATIFIES that matrix entry — it is the prior `assessment.json` (and therefore the daemon's `/api/catalog` projection, which still showed `partial/partial`) that was stale. After this write, the artifact and the matrix agree.
caveats: metric-drift-downstream: SubagentCount(parent) currently includes the background child — ComputeSubagentSummary (core/domain/session/grouped.go) has no background-vs-foreground flag to subtract, so signal #3 ('count excludes the background child') is not met. A future enhancement would tag the child's SessionState with a background flag derived from the transcript and subtract it from the parent's count.

feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant (inverted): the daemon HOLDS the parent in working until all linked children finish (reevaluateParent/hasActiveChildren), the opposite of the spec's signal #1 (parent goes ready while the child keeps running). The current model treats every linked child as foreground; producing the 'parent ready, child still working' shape would need the daemon to recognize a background dispatch and release the parent's turn.

The installed binary at assessment time is claude 2.1.150; the SendMessage primitive was introduced in 2.1.77 (per the surviveTurnDone comment, commit f15ed63). Verdicts assume a 2.1.77+ binary — an older Claude Code without SendMessage would revert agent_supports toward the prior partial.
Cloud/hosted long-running agents are a separate scenario (cloud-background-agent) and not this surface.

sources.note: strings scan of claude 2.1.150: 'run_in_background: true runs the agent asynchronously; you'll be notified when it completes', 'Execution: Direct / Task agent / Teammate (agent with true parallelism and inter-agent communication)', SendMessageTool with to: addressing, InProcessBackend.sendMessage(), 'Idle teammates can receive messages'. Confirms the fire-and-forget background-subagent primitive the prior assessment said was absent.

surviveTurnDone() includes SendMessage (added commit f15ed63, 2026-04-26) with comment 'Claude Code 2.1.77 replaced Agent({resume}) with SendMessage({to})... the sub-agent runs in the background' — irrlicht's own code confirms the upstream primitive.

reevaluateParent + hasActiveChildren HOLD the parent in working until children finish (the inverse of the spec's parent-goes-ready signal); SubagentQuietWindow guard in finishOrphanedChildren explicitly avoids promoting a still-writing background child. Background-subagent lifecycle is handled, but the distinguishing transition is not produced.

ComputeSubagentSummary sets Total = inProcess + len(fileChildren) and counts every ParentSessionID-linked child — no background-vs-foreground flag, so SubagentCount(parent) does NOT exclude a running background child (spec signal #3 unmet).

TestSessionDetector_BackgroundSubagentsNotFastForwarded (line 1947): 'parent must be held in working because the child is still active' — codifies the parent-held model that is the inverse of the background-subagent signal.

Source = FilesUnderRoot{transcriptsDir()}; the fswatcher discovers a background child's subagents/agent-*.jsonl transcript and links it by ParentSessionID independently of the parent's turn — discovery and linkage are observed.
CountOpenSubagents (line 553) returns 0 — file-based child sessions are the single source of truth for the count; no in-process supplement, and no background distinction.
features.subagents: true — the capability key for the 'subagents + background dispatch' primitive; flat features map, already correct.

Canonical spec. User-observable signals: (1) parent working→ready at turn end while child unchanged; (2) child disappears within readyTTL; (3) SubagentCount(parent) excludes the background child. Primitive exercised: subagents + background dispatch (SendMessage).
